semanticCore: Measuring volume, dry measure, portion, or load
quranicRelevance: Measuring volume of food/grains (Kayl - 'wa awfoo al-kayla'), and portion/camel load ('kayla ba'eer').

keyDerivations.meaning: To measure (by volume), weigh out, or receive measure
Measure, volume measure, portion, or load

figurativeOrRareUsages: كَيْلٌ يَسِيرٌ (12:65): An easy measure / small portion.
وَإِذَا كَالُوهُمْ أَوْ وَزَنُوهُمْ يُخْسِرُونَ: And when they measure/weigh for them, they cause loss.
discardedNoise: Grammatical classification of units of volume.
summaryEn: The root represents volumetric measurement, defining transactional honesty in trade weights (Kayl) and portion allotments in Egypt's famine distribution.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الكيل القياسي للحجم، محدداً الأمانة التجارية في الميزان (كيل)، وحصص الميرة الغذائية الموزعة في مصر.
rawText: اِكْتَالَ عَلَيْهِ 8 and : see عَلَى in the sense of مِنْ. كَيَّالٌ [A measurer of corn and the like]. (A, art. بخس.) كَيْلٌ : see مِكْيَالٌ. مِكْيَالٌ A measure with which corn is measured; (S, Msb, K;) as also 🡻كَيْلٌ; (Msb;) a measure of capacity.
